articles.title: Scorching Saturday: Mercury breaches 40 mark in season’s first - The Times of India
articles.full_text:
Chandigarh: The Tricity experienced its first major heat spike of the season on Friday as day temperatures breached the 40 degrees Celsius mark, officially marking the first heatwave day of the year. A heatwave is declared when maximum temperature reaches 40 degrees or more and stays 4.5 degrees to 6.4 degrees above normal.
According to the Chandigarh meteorological centre, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 40 degrees Celsius, which was 5 degrees Celsius above normal. Minimum temperature settled at 19.4 degrees Celsius, two degrees below the seasonal average.
Meteorological data shows that for the past five consecutive years, April has recorded temperatures crossing the 40 degrees Celsius threshold. Last year, highest maximum temperature in April was 41.5 degrees Celsius on April 28, while in 2024, the mercury touched 42.2 degrees Celsius on April 27. The all-time highest maximum temperature for April in Chandigarh remains 42.2 degrees Celsius, recorded in 2022.
What stands out this year, however, is the sharp temperature fluctuation earlier in the month. On April 8, Chandigarh recorded its second coldest April day since 1969, with maximum temperature dropping to just 24.2 degrees Celsius.
From that low, the city has now seen steep rise in temperature, indicating that hot summer conditions have firmly set in.
Officials said increasing temperatures signal a transition to sustained heat after a relatively moderate start to April. "Dry winds are prevailing, due to which humidity is present in the air, leading to a gradual rise in temperatures. These conditions are expected to persist and may cause heatwave conditions in other parts of the Tricity over the next two to three days," said Surender Paul, director, Chandigarh Meteorological Centre.
The weather department has forecast that the heat will continue over the coming days, with maximum temperatures expected to range between 38 degrees Celsius and 41 degrees Celsius over the next five days. Minimum temperatures are likely to hover between 20 degrees Celsius and 22 degrees Celsius. The sky will be mainly clear to partly cloudy, with no significant rainfall activity.
Residents have been advised to take precautions against heat exposure, particularly during peak afternoon hours.

articles.title: IMD Weather Alert: Heat Wave To Intensify In Delhi, Punjab; Rain And Storms Likely Across South And North East India - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
IMD Weather Alert: Severe heat waves continue to grip many states across India. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a gradual increase of 3-5 degrees Celsius in maximum temperatures over Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, and Delhi, while no significant change is expected over the rest of northwest India. In eastern India, maximum temperatures are likely to increase by 2-3 degrees Celsius over the next few days, indicating the continuation of the current heat wave in the region.
Rain is also likely to occur in the northeastern states during this period, which include Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura.
Rain accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds is expected in parts of central India, including Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, and Madhya Pradesh. Similar weather is also expected in southern India, including Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Rayalaseema, and Kerala.
Weather Over The Next 2 Days
A heat wave is expected in many areas of Jharkhand and Odisha on April 20. Coastal areas will experience intense heat and humidity. Nights are also expected to be unusually warm in parts of Odisha. In northeastern India, heavy rainfall is expected in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, and Meghalaya.
There may be thunderstorms and rain accompanied by lightning in some parts of Karnataka, Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, West Bengal, Sikkim and Telangana.
Heatwave conditions are likely to persist in Jharkhand, Odisha, and Rajasthan on April 21. Karnataka and Tamil Nadu will experience heat and high humidity. Heavy rain is expected again in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, and Meghalaya.
Thunderstorms are possible in some areas of Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Maharashtra, West Bengal, and Sikkim. Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, Goa, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura are at risk of lightning.
On Friday, several cities in Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Telangana, and Chhattisgarh recorded temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius. The worst heat waves were experienced in Rajnandgaon in Chhattisgarh and Wardha in Maharashtra, where the mercury reached 45 degrees Celsius.
Ratlam and Umaria in Madhya Pradesh also made the list of the top 10 hottest cities. Ratlam recorded a temperature of 44 degrees Celsius, while Umaria recorded 43.8 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, School timings have been changed in 13 districts of Chhattisgarh due to the intense heat.

articles.title: Chandigarh Weather Update: भीषण गर्मी की आहट! चंडीगढ़-पंचकूला में 37 डिग्री पार, जानें अब कब होगी बारिश? - Zee News
articles.full_text:
Chandigarh Weather Update: चंडीगढ़ में एक बार फिर गर्मी ने रफ्तार पकड़ ली है. पिछले दिनों मौसम में हल्का बदलाव देखने को मिला था, लेकिन अब तापमान लगातार बढ़ रहा है, जिससे लोगों को परेशानी होने लगी है. मौसम विभाग के अनुसार 19 अप्रैल से 24 अप्रैल तक मौसम पूरी तरह ड्राई बना रहेगा.
चंडीगढ़ में गर्मी ने बढ़ते ही जा ही है. कुछ दिन पहले मौसम थोड़ा बदला भी था, लेकिन अब दोबारा मौसम गर्म होते जा रहा है. यहां तापमान हर दिन बढ़ते ही जा रहा है. लोगों को गर्मी से परेशानी होना भी शुरू हो गया है. मौसम विभाग के मुताबिक आज 19 अप्रैल से लेकर 24 अप्रैल तक मौसम ड्राई रहेगा. वहीं, मौसम विभाग ने लोगों को अलर्ट रहने के साथ ही खाने पीने पर ध्यान देने की बात कहीं.
चंडीगढ़ में आज अधिकतम तापमान 38 डिग्री तक रहने की संभावना है, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान 21 डिग्री के आसपास है. वही IMD ने 19 अप्रैल से लेकर 24 अप्रैल तक मौसम ड्राई होने वाला है. बढ़ते गर्मी में लोगों मौसम विभाग ने लोगों को सावधान रहने के लिए कहा है. बता दें कि अगले 35 तारीख तक तो कोई बारिश को अलर्ट नहीं है.
वहीं चंडीगढ़ के पास स्थित पंचकूला और मोहाली भी तापमान हर रोज बढ़ ही रहा है. यहां भी लोगों को गर्मी परेशान कर रही है. पंचकूला में भी आज धिकतम तपमान 38 डिग्री तक रहने वाला है, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान 21 डिग्री तक रह सकता है. इन दोनों जिलों में भी 24 तारीख तक मौसम ड्राई रहने की संभावना है.
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, आने वाले दिनों में मौसम शुष्क बना रहेगा. विभाग के डायरेक्टर सुरेंद्र पाल ने बताया कि अगले 24 घंटों में अधिकतम तापमान में कोई बड़ा बदलाव नहीं देखने को मिलेगा.
हालांकि इसके बाद तापमान में धीरे-धीरे बढ़ोतरी होने की संभावना है. अनुमान है कि पारा 2 से 3 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक बढ़ सकता है, जिससे गर्मी का असर और ज्यादा महसूस होगा.
